name: cksum
readme:
Short:        Display file checksums and sizes (FreeBSD)
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       The Regents of the University of California (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.0

cksum - Display file checksums and sizes (FreeBSD)

Original author: The Regents of the University of California
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  cksum [-o 1|2|3] [file ...]
  sum [-r] [-o 1|2|3] [file ...]

  Computes and displays CRC checksum and byte count for each file.
  Supports multiple algorithm variants via -o flag.

Options:
  -o 1    BSD sum (16-bit, 512-byte blocks)
  -o 2    SysV sum (16-bit, 512-byte blocks)
  -o 3    32-bit CRC (default for cksum)

Examples:
  cksum file.txt          ; CRC32 checksum and byte count
  cksum -o 1 file.txt     ; BSD sum algorithm
  sum file.txt            ; synonym for cksum -o 1
  cksum *                 ; checksum all files in directory

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Multi-file port (cksum.c, crc.c, crc32.c, sum.c) from FreeBSD. Higher stack (64KB) needed for CRC table initialization. 37 tests cover all three algorithm modes (-o 1/2/3), binary files, empty files, multi-file checksumming, and stdin mode.
